primary_accounting_issue: Whether an intragroup dividend receivable creating foreign currency risk exposure can be designated as a hedged item in consolidated financial statements

primary_authority.reason: Paragraph 6.3.5 establishes the general requirement for external party transactions as hedged items, while paragraph 6.3.6 provides the specific exception for intragroup monetary items denominated in foreign currency
Interpretation specifically addresses hedge accounting for net investments in foreign operations, including identification of eligible hedged risks and where hedging instruments can be held

supporting_authority.reason: Paragraphs 15 and 15A define monetary items receivable from foreign operations and paragraph 45 addresses how exchange differences on intragroup monetary items are recognized in consolidated financial statements

peripheral_authority.reason: Disclosures related to hedge accounting but not directly governing the eligibility of intragroup monetary items as hedged items
Hedge accounting effectiveness requirements but not directly addressing the core issue of intragroup item eligibility
Additional guidance on reclassification of exchange differences on disposal and net investment hedge mechanics, not on initial eligibility of intragroup items
Translation of foreign operations and disposal accounting, not directly relevant to hedge designation eligibility

treatment_families.family: Eligibility of hedged items for hedge accounting under IFRS 9
Recognition of foreign exchange gains/losses on intragroup monetary items in consolidation

approaches.label: General external party restriction
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.5 generally requires hedged items to be with parties external to the reporting entity, which would exclude intragroup dividend receivables from being designated as hedged items in consolidated financial statements

approaches.label: Intragroup monetary item exception for foreign currency risk
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.6 provides an explicit exception allowing the foreign currency risk of intragroup monetary items to qualify as a hedged item in consolidated financial statements when the exposure affects consolidated profit or loss
